faqs.question: What are Aligners?
faqs.answer: Aligners are clear, removable trays custom-made to gradually shift your teeth into the desired position. They are virtually invisible and a comfortable alternative to traditional braces.

faqs.question: How do Aligners work?
faqs.answer: You wear a series of aligner trays, each slightly different, for about 10 days before moving to the next set. Over time, your teeth move into alignment as guided by your orthodontist's treatment plan.

faqs.question: Can any dentist do irregular teeth treatment?
faqs.answer: Orthodontic treatment requires specialized training. At Whistle, all treatments are led by experienced orthodontists at Clove Dental clinics to ensure safe and effective results.

faqs.question: Are there any restriction on eating or drinking?
faqs.answer: You should remove your aligners before eating or drinking anything other than water. This helps maintain aligner clarity and prevents damage to the trays.

faqs.question: How long does the treatment take?
faqs.answer: Treatment duration varies based on case complexity, typically ranging from 6 to 18 months. Your orthodontist will provide a personalized timeline after your 3D scan and consultation.
